nodes.label: AI as test management and reporting tool
nodes.grounding_span: AI primarily handles test management and reporting where it can handle large volumes of data at a time

nodes.label: Human judgment in visual and navigational testing
nodes.grounding_span: issues can sometimes be visual and the navigating of the application is better done by humans

nodes.label: AI as collaborative heavy lifter under human direction
nodes.grounding_span: AI just does the heavy lifting by putting it all together

nodes.label: AI efficiency for test managers vs manual burden for test associates
nodes.label_negative: Equitable distribution of AI benefits across roles
nodes.grounding_span: Test Managers, with the help of AI, can work more efficiently...Test Associates might feel like they do more work but for less pay

nodes.label: AI as optional tool used only when beneficial
nodes.label_negative: AI as mandatory or forced tool
nodes.grounding_span: We are not required to use AI if it makes things harder

nodes.label: AI following predetermined workflows
nodes.grounding_span: AI can get to a point where it can run, test, and report all on its own but only when it follows a predetermined workflow

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: this process can be done quicker, allowing more time to be spent on testing

nodes.label: Human expertise and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: it still requires thorough planning from a human to execute effectively

nodes.label: Fairness and role equity
nodes.grounding_span: Test Associates might feel like they do more work but for less pay than a Test Manager

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as efficiency booster
nodes.grounding_span: With the help of AI this process can be done quicker

nodes.label: Concern about inequitable workload distribution
nodes.grounding_span: Test Associates might feel like they do more work but for less pay than a Test Manager

nodes.label: Confidence in human planning irreplaceability
nodes.grounding_span: it still requires thorough planning from a human to execute effectively

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI's ability to test freely
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think it will ever be capable of testing a piece of software freely and thoroughly without guidance

nodes.label: Role-differentiated reasoning — evaluates AI impact separately for different job roles
nodes.grounding_span: Test Managers, with the help of AI, can work more efficiently...Test Associates might feel like they do more work

nodes.label: Pragmatic conditional adoption — uses AI only when it clearly improves workflow
nodes.grounding_span: We are not required to use AI if it makes things harder
